zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem II van Lijnden於1165年出生在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德布倫連登時，他的父親Floris Van Lijnden15歲，母親Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck15歲。他和Christine Brederode至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1227年7月27日在荷蘭武特雷特武特雷特享年62歲，埋葬在荷蘭武特雷特武特雷特。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Lijnden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): plural form of   Linde  .
Flemish and Dutch (Van Linden): habitational name from any of the places called Linden or Lienden in Brabant and North Brabant.
Flemish and Dutch: shortened form of   Vanderlinden  .

variants.name: Linde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Gunner, Lars.  German Kurt, Dieter, Erna, Fritz, Hasso, Markus, Otto, Ralf.

German, Dutch, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived by a conspicuous lime tree, Middle High German, Dutch linde, Scandinavian lind. There are several places, especially in northern Germany, named with this word, and the name may be a habitational name from any of these. The word was also used in a number of Old High German women's personal names, with the meaning ‘shield’ or ‘spear’ (these being made from the hard wood of the lime); it is possible that the surname in some cases is from a short form of one of these. As a Swedish name it is often ornamental rather than topographic. Compare Lindy .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Linde ‘lime tree’ (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem II van Lijnden was born in 1165, in Lienden, Buren, Gelderland, Netherlands, his father, Floris Van Lijnden, was 15 and his mother, Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck, was 15. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Christine Brederode. He died on 27 July 1227, in Utrecht, Utrecht, Netherlands, at the age of 62, and was buried in Utrecht, Utrecht, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem II van Lijnden est né en 1165, à Lienden, Buren, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris Van Lijnden, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Christine Brederode. Il est décédé le 27 juillet 1227, à Utrecht, Utrecht, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Utrecht, Utrecht, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem II van Lijnden 1165 in Lienden, Buren, Geldern, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Floris Van Lijnden 15 und seine Mutter Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck 15. Er hatte mit Christine Brederode mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Juli 1227 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Utrecht, Utrecht, Niederlande und wurde in Utrecht, Utrecht, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem II van Lijnden è nato il nel 1165 nel Lienden, Buren, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Floris Van Lijnden, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Christine Brederode. Morì il 27 luglio 1227, nel Utrecht, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Utrecht, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Willem II van Lijndenが1165年にオランダジェルダーランドブレンリーンデンで生まれた時，父Floris Van Lijndenは15歳，母Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonckは15歳であった。彼はChristine Brederodeとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1227年7月27日にオランダウトレチトウトレチトで62歳で死去し，オランダウトレチトウトレチトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem II van Lijnden(이)가 1165년 네덜란드 글델랜드 버 리든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Floris Van Lijnden(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Christine Brederode(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1227년 7월 27일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 우트레ch트에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 우트레ch트 우트레ch트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem II van Lijnden nasceu em 1165, em Lienden, Buren, Gheldria, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris Van Lijnden, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Christine Brederode. Ele faleceu em 27 de julho de 1227, em Utrecht, Utrecht, Países Baixos, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Utrecht, Utrecht, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem II van Lijnden родился в 1165 г. в местности лиенден, бурен, джелдирланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Floris Van Lijnden было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck, 15. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Christine Brederode. Он умер 27 июля 1227 г. в местности утречт, утречт, Нидерланды в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности утречт, утречт, Нидерланды.
